comments: Aaron is very solid. On time, attentive, pleasant.
Super smooth in everything he does.
Very very very solid back. Only minor issues to be more aggressive for middle balls and sometimes has tracking issues on faults/end balls that end up near him.
test
Andrea is great. She is a very smart back. She is very aware of her surroundings. Balances well and is good about looking around to "read" what's going on.
Good shift. Nothing of note
Solid and always making the right choice
Day 3 - good job working around player getting towel. She’s looking very comfortable

Quite a good back. Knows when to roll and what side the balls should be one. Knows what's happening and when she's on/off. Steps in to avoid rolling into the player's feet. Solid performance, ok rolls.
Decent first day as a net - has good speed and effort. Forgot a match point but realized it right afterwards. Some iffy rolls

Solid speed and effort. Stellar management of tiebreaker during first shift of knowing where serve is and being decisive on how many to roll. Broke freeze frame when ball was hit in net in front of him but realized before he picked it up

Did a pretty good job Saturday, especially when he took over for me at net when I started getting gassed. Doesn't have the initiative or confidence to be a captain just yet but he helped his rookie back partner a good deal and covered up some of the rookie mistakes
Good speed and leadership on court
Day 3 - Andrew is a stud. Quick, decisive, good hands, doesn’t make mistakes. Great bCk
Very good effort. Just needs a bit more "court time" but very effective. Tries hard, wants to learn.
Indecisive about when to cross and when to grab a ball on the other side of the net. Otherwise great attitude and tries hard

Andrew is having a little trouble with decision making as a net. Has trouble remember score and dosnt have the best awareness. Seems to take improvement suggestions well but has trouble implementing them. Might be better as a back

Shift 1 Grandstand - good hustle. Needs to step out to balance.
Shift 2 court 5 - corrected himself
Shift 3 Harris - showing hands after every point 
Shift 4 court 4 - nothing bad
Shift 5 Stadium - nothing bad
Shift 6 Grandstand - good hustle. Good hands. Shows hands after every point. Good job finding a ball. Waited for point to be over to look. Good freeze frame.

Overall - improved a lot over the day
Solid for his first year. Needs to be a bit more aware on court to be quicker

Andrew was also a pretty good rookie. Good attitude and really good on court! You can tell he’s lacking some experience, as it doesn’t seem like he (or most of the nets I’ve had) is super quick with his decisions (mostly it’s just not an immediate reaction like for the vets for things like game changes). Didn’t see any real mess ups or anything I thought he did poorly!
One atrocious roll but otherwise solid shift
Really solid rook. Seeing really great speed and effort and attentiveness to player
Good back and volunteered gladly to stay a shift!!!
Good net for a first year. Could be a little more aggressive but that will come with time. Just a little hesitant, but understandable for his first day.
Moves ok but a bit slow on decision making.

Definitely got better throughout the day. But he’s pretty shaky on what he’s supposed to be doing, and even shaky in the rules of tennis. He asked multiple times how scoring works, tiebreakers, etc. Doesn’t make the greatest decisions on when to cross the net, roll, etc.
Decent net but on the slower side. Very aware of what is going on. Reminded me of ball change that was coming!
Missed a ball in back when he came back from other side back, then missed another on next point. He’s been ok but there are some significant errors, not usually happening that often tho
Seems to know what to do just not automatic yet. He was my back partner today

Day 3 - was my back partner for a shift. Was aggressive with balls in middle, quick, seemed very comfortable and aware. Getting better at balancing and anticipating player needs throughout day. Above average rookie back
Active and alert. Always showing hands
Generally good things. Communicative with nets on when to roll vs not. Good speed. Straight arms

Kid always gives it his all, is professional on and off the court, and is on top of everything. Also works with his teammate well to help them improve (was paired with a decent second year). Impressive stuff outta him and a joy to have on the team
Legum was a big help today. I had him step in and lead my nets. He had good input in the captains meetings.
Runs hard and fast! A great net with some sweet flow
Day 3 - Legum looked excellent today, best I’ve seen him. Very decisive
Basically peak of powers Ashton now. Is on top of everything. Still quick. Good hands. Great judgment.
Everyone knows this, but this kid is just awesome. Superb effort. Only dinging him from 5 stars cause i think he went to back one time when he didn’t need to.

Another great shift out of Ashton. Has the speed the quickness to make things look so smooth. With his speed he tries to go for almost every ball but sometimes needs to hold it back a bit (esp for doubles). Seemed a little slower to react and understand what was happening during a supertiebreak.
Has some of the best anticipation I’ve seen. Regularly gets balls that are coming into net after point, before they hit the net. Covers rebounds with 100% reliability.

I knew this day was coming, I am officially conceding that Ashton is a better net than I am. His rolls are better than most nets which really differentiates him from some other top nets, they’re flatter and he controls speed well depending on the situation. He’s fast, makes great decisions, etc… I think Ashton is mens singles final quality

Good improvement during the day (upward trajectory). Handled first tiebreaker today as a rookie, had a few mishaps/confusion on which direction on where to roll the ball but self-corrected later in the tiebreak. Could continue to work on ball awareness but overall great athleticism. Solid rolls
High potential rookie. Lozanova smoothed out his initial struggles
Having a pretty good shift. Has nice rolls and great speed. Doesn’t always have the best awareness
Good hustle and good speed. Was too aggressive jumping in to grab balls during warmup. Good on ball changes and games ending but was slow to get ball in serving side at start of match

Good speed and enthusiasm. But he sometimes forgets what he supposed to do. He rolled to the wrong side I think a couple times today, he made some questionable decisions on when to cross the net, and he’s sometimes slow on the serving side switch.
Was the best of all rookie net crew, but still some moments of hesitation + cueing off vet Backs
Quite a capable first-year net. Very good hustle and anticipation, and nice court awareness
Nice guy. Very pleasant. Not real quick. Needs some more court time to know the what, when, where, and how. (But good guy and tried.)
Rolled to the non serving side once on stadium
Kind of slow on decision making

Tuesday 8/2
rolled cross court. Did not look both ways after crossing court. left receiving side with a ball.
Rough warmup, crossed to same side as other net, held ball aloft over his head for about 20 seconds before eventually rolling it after a minute
Having a really solid all around shift. Supportive to his net partner
test
Improved. One missed ball
Day 4 - missed a ball with a back, never looked. Still hesitating
Bumping up to 3.5
Showing good speed today
Good awareness noticing ball roll to net after bouncing off back walll
Completely spaced out when the balls were switching sides. The back had to run up to the net and roll the balls to the serving side back

Ok first day. Bouncy rolls and not always sure which way to roll the balls. Did ok on a 20 point tiebreak but I had to direct him frequently which way the balls went. He did start to pick up the pattern after 12 points. Good effort
Too much poaching. Bouncy rolls. Lawrenz must fix
Hyper fast. Can get himself in trouble with overactiveness but largely locked in
Generally pretty quick and always looks both ways after each point
Good communication with the backs to roll or not roll
He’s ok, but is trying to do way more than the 101 stuff and isn’t quite getting it. Poaching balls too much, holding a ball at net for no real reason, etc
Crossed on a service side change when other net clearly could not switch to other side (getting ball in alley). Left back stranded with 3 balls to deliver.
Excellent captain. Takes her time with explanations and always plays it by the book. Will still have an occasional bobble but that's it
Good effort as a rookie net. Seemed solid. Could have looked a little bit more to show what he had in tiebreaker
Connor was super. Has worked IW for a number of years. Court smart and very fast.

My only real nit is a tough one- he’s just a little too active. Covers for balls that are way outside his territory. In some sense, he’s doing what we asked. But lacks that nuanced sense of when/when not to act.
Mistaken rolling decision
Really really good
Really good. Also will note he said he’s been a ballkid at indian wells/ miami/ and san jose so he’s got some previous experience anyways. Did nice job on stadium during rublev match.

I think he's getting a little confused by what nets are supposed to do at the other tournament he works because hes just a step slow in figuring out what balls he should go for and when he should be crossing. Otherwise he did a good job

Wednesday 8/3
Shift 2 Harris - forgot which way the ball goes after game
Shift 3 Court 4 - forgot which side balls were going in tiebreak. Good freeze frame.
Seems much more decisive and faster after a few days in our system
Awesome!!!
Really gives max effort
Deb was a big help today. Great job working with the rookies

Sunday 7/31
Shift 1 Stadium - good hustle. Getting to balls faster than a 14 year old
Shift 2 grandstand - cactus arms
Shift 3 Harris - held freeze frame
Excellent coverage on tough situations. Great teammate
Puts in a good effort but limited physically. Rolls can be of questionable quality.
Solid back. Good ball awareness (when to hold v. when to roll) but could check in his feet a bit more. Good effort and solid team member. Saw some effective balancing. Great rolls
Definite improvement from last year. Has some discombobulations because it looks like he's still figuring out his new lankiness

Dhruv is enthusiastic but oddly loud in his running. His feet/footsteps are extremely loud. Another team captain actually mentioned it to me...thinking he was doing it intentionally. He is fairly fast but rather spastic and obvious in his movement. He does not blend in. 
Decent back but needs to appear more in control.
Knows what he’s doing, just sometimes a little clunky.
Did better Monday about being more in control when running, etc.

Tuesday 8/2
Shift 2 Stadium - good hustle.
Shift 3 grandstand - good hustle. Good freeze frame.
Overall good back. needs to improve on when he should be crossing with his back partner.
Day 3 - Only saw a handful of games and didn’t have anything overly challenging happen but he looks very comfortable, hasn’t put a foot wrong. Excellent snag on grandstand.
was paired with Stu and still had a fine first day as captain
Ella is a very strong back. Very nice kid. Listens, tries, and is aware of what is going on around her. Tries hard.

She struggled to realize when balls needed to go to the other side but followed her fellow back quickly. Messed up the back rotation. Good at balancing. Not holding up the court but not great. Super nice and wants to learn!
Good recognition on last ball in play. Was part of situation where player picked up balls themselves after changeover
Just had a weird situation with a player dropping a ball and she perfectly read what the player wanted. Hard to explain but really well anticipated
She’s pretty good. Not great at balancing though. And speed is a bit lacking. But learns  quickly and is smart so that’s a big plus.
Excellent shift all thru with fellow rook

High effort, really good rookie. Figuring out when she can leave the player to quickly scoop up a ball Backs also on this shift were Stu/Judie/Amelie and another rookie. She did pretty well in a tough spot considering how difficult it is to balance with that group.

Not the worst rookie net I've seen. Looked both ways after nearly every point, but looked way behind when Jeff Zhang was his net partner on the last shift of the day (which makes sense, it's Jeff Zhang we're talking about). Sluggish at times but that might be from hesitation

Sunday 7/31
Shift 1 Stadium - did not run across the net when net partner ran across
Shift 2 Grandstand - held onto ball when had plenty of time to roll. Unsure which side to roll to
Shift 3 Harris - not enough time on court to eval

Eshan was also pretty good for a rookie net. Needs to be a little more ready for those change of games (like all of the nets) but he put in a lot of effort, and generally knew what was happening. He has a good attitude. Needs to get a little quicker or more efficient with rolling ball in hand, then looking to receiving side to get the ball from the back. Said he’s really enjoying and is definitely coming back next year to be a ballkid again!
test
Seeing tremendous growth since yesterday. Taking command of tie break helping guide the backs
Very good awareness during warm ups
Seems pretty aware and active. Main con is just speed and athleticism.
I don’t think he’s quick enough. But boy he tries hard - very good effort. And is pretty aware/active. I would not be that worried about putting him on court
Moves a little slowly but good attitude.
Great job making it this far. Mild hesitation and tends to delegate decisions to the vet nets he's with, which makes sense given his rookie status

8/1: my best back yesterday. Stellar performance. Took Luke (his back partner) under his wing and mentored him throughout the day. Also did a great job helping organize the backs and offered feedback to the entire team during our debrief sessions after. Great communicater and team member. Felt like I could rely on him. One of my dedicated backs to hold one ball on the receiving side during the tiebreaker.
Don't put as net. Not that good as net during tiebreak. Broke freeze frame at super tiebreak.
Great mentor for captain. Super helpful to lean on today as co-captain
So committed to serving the player first that he just had a ricochet hit him in the face. He barely flinched. What a pro
Took new rookie backs under wing
Messed up a freeze frame on our second shift. Redeemed himself on the third though
Improved from day 1
Pretty good speed and hands overall. Awareness could improve
Evan very fast and good effort. Needs more time to build confidence but very good.

This guy went to Towson so he gets bonus points. Besides that, he also is generally one of the best rookie backs this year from what I've seen. Good aggressiveness, nice rolls, usually serves the player first, and has a decent idea of what's going on at any given time. I could see him making Friday
He makes a lot of mistakes. But he also knows right away that they were mistakes. So it seems like he knows what he’s doing, but unfortunately makes pretty frequent errors.
It's all going to come together for him at some point, hopefully Costanza can unlock it. Good aside from thinking about rolling between serves
Day 3 - dude is going hard! Serious effort retrieving balls

Overall great day. Shows that he knows what he us doing and anticipating what to do next. Did an excellent job in a 10 point tiebreak. Great balancing. Starting to work on more advanced skill such as communicating and holding the last ball in play down
Breakthrough day. Flawless with Carolyn. Asked question after shift
Shows really great speed and effort consistently
Not bad for rookie, but doesn’t stand out in any way from what I’ve seen right now.
Day 3 - slow and tentative. Had a surprisingly solid match tiebreak, but too many mistakes throughout the day.
Weak hands, doesn’t really come out from spot. Positioned herself in odd spot. Very slow to recognize service side change.
Def the weakest back on this team. Doesn’t look to balance unless she has 4 balls. General awareness hasn’t been great.
High effort but not all clicking
Slow and tentative in everything she does.
Day 4 - Back partnered with faith today. She is getting slightly better from when I saw her last, but she’s hesitant, rolled a ball the wrong way, not really balancing. Also brought phone to court.
Great team member and great performance today. Offered feedback which was useful to helping improve overall team performance.

Sunday 7/31
Overall average. Nothing crazy good or bad
Good performance today at net. Good but not great speed but has good court sense for what is going on and what he should be doing. Missed the freeze frame on our last match of the day
Good first day, good job helping teach a rookie back. Did need to work on awarness, especially at ball switch. Good balancing
Good second day. Partnered with a rookie back. Did keep balancing when his back partner wasnt ready. Did tell him he needs to wait. Needs to work on communication
Really solid and impressive. Only issue was not as assertive on one middle ball than I would like, but he did a cross with Shawn, and overall made good decisions. No lazy cactus from him!
Always holds one ball down, whether it was in play or not. Kind of walks a LOT towards player when giving ball. A few steps is right, halfway across the court is not.
Very solid. Haven’t really seen him do anything wrong. Building off a strong rookie year this year
Really good at showing hands after every point, finishing runs, is aggressive and doesn’t watch a rolling ball (takes the 3 is better than none seriously)
Good overall athleticism. Generally knows what to do on court but could be more assertive in trying to help out his back partner or rolling the ball to the net (esp during tiebreakers)

Day 4 - really good second year back. I need to see him be a little more agressive on catching and and getting lose balls a little faster when also serving the player to bump him up to an excellent 2nd year but he’s really good
Kid is really good. Makes himself known on court and steps up from position well so the player can see him and ball people can roll/even balls.
This is a very good first year back. Covers well for others, makes good decisions on court. Seems to just get what needs to happen.
Good court awareness and decision making.

Impressed with him for a second year. He does the main things right and is very professional on court. Always in the right place, looked both ways, good with players (fan, water, umbrella, etc). Only thing is taking those smaller things to the next step (anticipate next ball change, knowing he has a little more time to roll when the player is getting ready to serve, etc). I could tell he is starting to know where all balls are at all times (his shoe was once untied and instead of fixing it he ran to help a back get a ball that was stuck under a chair that they didn’t see- due to the player hitting it there. Don’t worry, he tied his shoe after the next point).
Need to do a reminder about the phone thing again. He was not bad today. Did miss a freeze frame, which happens. Did a nice job in the tiebreaker today though.

Shift 1 Grandstand - good awareness. Bouncy rolls. Poaches
Shift 2 court 5 - was on the same side as other net on even game ball change
Shift 3 Harris -struggles with umbrella
Shift 4 court 4 - hesitate a lot. Held on to ball when he had plenty of time to roll
Solid first year, needs to be a little better about looking both ways
Quick and decisive but sometimes looks a little awkward on court.  Tried hard.
Great effort and speed
Had a lapse in judgement rolling as a player was already at the line but immediately followed it up with a great effort
Only had for one shift that was her first one, showed improvement through shift on moving balls from one side to other. Struggled on serve switching sides and with catching rolls. Solid effort
Much improved from yesterday. Solid hustle, mostly knew where to go during tiebreaker
Hustling very hard. Doesn’t always recognize game changes right away and struggles dealing with multiple balls being rolled to her

Tries hard but is still working to process different situations in game which causes her to react slower. Had some trouble during the supertiebreak in figuring out which way the balls were supposed to go and making herself available to receive/roll balls. Improved from her first day but still has room to grow.
Like Dhruv, another big step from her rookie year. Solid, calm, composed. Worked well with Dhruv
Looking good, as she did last year. A few missed exchanges on balancing, but happens early in tiebreaker. Needs to communicate a bit more in tiebreaker. Pretty good though.
Stuck around for rain

She’s been good on all shifts. Significantly better than last year if I’m remembering correctly. Some complaints:

- She gets quite loud when sitting. On stadium her and her friends kept talking too loud after repeatedly being told to talk a bit quieter. To be fair, I think it was mostly her friends being loud, but she was sometimes as well. 
- She doesn’t seem to try to run very fast, sometimes a bit too casually going to pick up balls. She told me she doesn’t like run haha so that’s probably why. 

But overall I think she’s a good back. She was partnered with her friend and Imaan made them both look relatively good despite her friend being bad. Mostly I think speed is an issue.

Could have moved further from player to roll balls to net when switching sides but waited instead which delayed balls getting down when players were ready to go. Just required a little effort to move to the side
Gets lazy at times and whining about going out on court
Main issue is that she’s scared of the ball. Not very aggressive or reactive to loose balls either.
Athleticism/speed is a significant limit.
Moves slowly especially during warm up. Snarky and thinks she knows it all. Doesn’t listen. Difficult to work with.

balanced into fence when partner wasn’t ready and player was about to serve. Needs to step away from fence.
Showed up to wrong team. Low effort
Good job for first day. Eager to learn and improved through the day. Did have some trouble with decisionmaking but improved through the day

A good kid. Immanuella helped him tremendously throughout the day and made his hesitation less noticeable, but he had some issues with serving the player first instead of going for nearby balls. I think he'll continue to improve though
Locked in, aggressive. Alert

Excellent job with a ball that got lost on court 5. Persistent about looking for it without ever disrupting play, always stopped looking before players would serve. Looked to teammates for instructions on where it was (got stuck behind top of score board, impossible to get) Really impressed
Overall a great shift. Really focused on court and anticipating what to do next
Did a net-back switch during qualies confidently. Good decisiveness. Overall good performance, no glaring errors
Overall fine. Unchallenged, but error-free
Good back, bobbled a few balls but generally on top of things
Really good effort but ran up to net when back was considerably closer
As a veteran I was surprised by very bouncy rolls, not quick on decisions, and overall not as aware as I thought she’d be.
honestly would like to see him more as a back than a net. has the occasional bad roll

Ugh, she’s a fine net; but her rolls are just way too bouncy. It’s a serious problem on stadium when she rolls to a back treating a serving side player, leading the ball to bounce back off the wall almost half way to net several times. I know i have seen this in past years too, so something to keep an eye on in shifts on stadium other days.

Started the day not so hot. I was slightly underimpressed from her performance in the morning in terms of having a veteran. It wasn’t super clean and some of the awareness or decision-making was meh :/ but then later in the day she put in a lot more effort when we needed it and was doing better. It’s monday so, understandable
Helped her rookie every changeover

Appreciate the effort today but had a couple rough tiebreakers. Even after reviewing how a tiebreaker works the next shift wasnt much better. She did not know which way the balls needed to go and was carried by her net partner who ran the tiebreaker. It almost got to the point were I was going to swap positions with her but thankfully her other net kept things going
(This goes with the written eval above)
overall a solid 2nd year. his maturity shows on-court, masking his mistakes: he always knows to look the correct vet
He might not be the fastest net out there, but he rolls FAST and usually pretty good and accurate. Handled ball changes pretty well despite being paired with a rookie net all day
Mr. Us Open
This might be a breakthrough year for Jeremy. Smooth, fast and a good teammate for multiple rookies today
Really nice reflexes and did a nice job at the back with imam. A few rolling mishaps when balancing.
He moves around the back a lot, randomly. Was walking all the way to the other back when he had the server which I've never seen before. Otherwise decent hands, moves well

8/1: super eager/great intensity on court. Great hustle and good rolls. But sometimes almost too intense lol. He does a weird thing like circling around the player/walking almost next to the other back to serve the player. Emily let him know, think this has toned down for now but caught him doing it once or twice more afterwards. He's super with it, pretty much always knows what's going on and was one of my dedicated backs to hold one ball on the receiving side during the tiebreaker

Day 3 - love the aggression but moved slightly moved anticipating an ace by the player getting a racket on it. Then on stadium shift he caught a HUGE serve by Sock, must’ve been atleast mid 120s and maybe best catch I’ve ever seen. Possibly best hands we have? Rolling is a bit erratic

Jeremy is improving so much. Michael White believes he has the best hands of all the ballpeople (which is a stretch), but he's mostly sure-handed and has a pretty good idea of what to do at any given time
Day 4 - roll is very wristy and violent, causes it to be inconsistent and often wild. Still loving the high work rate, he’s going all out all the time
Helped out vol
Very aggressive and decisive. Good at balancing balls in the back too.
Needs to continue to work on ball awareness and assertiveness to getting towards the ball. A bit timid. Hopefully will loosen up/ improve with experience and time

She got the full Stu experience and actually handled it as well as expected for first day. Kept looking for balancing even though it looked like she might die of stagefright given everything she was dealing with

Sunday 7/31
Shift 1 Stadium - did not roll right after point ended
Shift 2 grandstand - needs to move forward from the fence when player is toweling to receive balls from net. Could not catch balls from net. Needs to move from fence in general
Shift 3 Harris - held freeze frame

In general - not much hustle. Deb was getting balls from her area
Jessica held up Jonah’s slack. She’s quite good and smart, and learns quickly.
Oops sorry wasn’t done writing yet. Speed and rolling power is an issue, but otherwise she’s very good. I don’t think I have any other complaints. I’m always comfortable with her on court.
A bit timid overall and has some trouble handling 3+ balls
Not great hands. Bobbling quite a few. Bounces to player are hit or miss on accuracy
Pleasure to be with John!!!
Wasn't as decisive in getting fault balls or helping out his partner in evening out the balls. Forgot to shift during service warmups.
Didn’t move to middle during serve warmups. Didn’t do great job with leading rookie partner on when to balance
I think he might be the worst overall ballkid to ever be on my team. Never has any clue what’s going on, asks the same questions over and over again, never learns, etc.
Definitely has some sort of learning disability. Unchallenged, but was great on balancing
Better today. Still not great, but wasn’t a big liability on court anymore.
Helped out vols

This kid was so annoying. Couldn’t believe he wasn’t a rookie. Literally asked if he could talk to players on changeover. Also tried to dictate shifts to captains. Then didn’t say anything before leaving and i saw him playing mini tennis
Good hustle. No major mistakes

Good job for a returning 2nd year. Was with a rookie back the entire day and definitely anchored that side of the court tremendously. Still needs some work on situational awareness but that'll come in time

Really sharp by end of day with putting last ball down and paying attention to player tendencies and how it affects balances. Solid hustle, was late getting out on a changeover so player had to pick balls up
Still seeing hesitation and lack of confidence
Rough bounces. Took three to get to player at the baseline
This might be too low, only saw 2 games of her. She looked good, very confident, quick to service player. Balanced well except for one that Debbie wasn’t ready for (maybe more Debbie’s fault)
Giving a 4 adjust my last rating to a 3.5 after watching more

Terrific back partner! I have seen a lot of improvement from Joy since last year, and honestly she was pretty good by the end of last year too. If we can make space for her on a finals team, I'd be delighted since she deserves it

Didn't have her for long on my team but generally needs to work on ball awareness, specifically looking around her feet at the net. Gave feedback directly to her, she took it well. Shift ended before she had a chance to implement. Great effort
mild upside here I think. The maturity helps...she's looking for feedback, watching everything. Some misses awareness wise
Slow rolls, many 2 bounce throws to the player. Fumbles a lot of balls. Great attitude and high effort
Worked on improving confidence throught the day. Still very unsure on court

Had more time to observe today and performance has not improved from yesterday. Great effort, asks questions. But tried to run back on court during a point after getting a ball that got hit to the side. Still needs to work on ball awareness.

Not great bounces so if we can somehow work on that, but she always asks GREAT and productive questions. Actually listens and takes what I say after each debrief into action (even immediately which was nice to see). Seems to catch on quickly so definitely like having her on my team

Very slow rolls. Told her to try scooping the ball with the palm up but continued to try to stop the ball with the palm down. Was shocked when I said I noticed she fumbles with the balls (I guess she thinks she's not fumbling the balls?). Frustrating to watch but at least sometimes looks down by her feet for balls. Serves the player first which is good and grabs the ball if her back partner is busy with the player
Seeing some general lack of decisiveness and uncertainty
Had a fine shift. Nothing Of note either way
Kayla is enthusiastic but perhaps a bit "scattered". Got better as day went on. Typical 15 year old....phone, forgot time to meet, but overall nice kid.
Balancing but slow and risky balances that could easily pause play
Listened and wanted to learn.  Overall good first year.
Has gotten better as the week has gone on.
Okay all-around first day. Paired with Gates so he makes everyone look competent.
Decent shifts today. Good effort today and did a good job in 10 point tiebreak. Does need to work on awarness as she missed several balancing rolls from different back partners
Not a lot of speed or effort being shown on stadium
Not putting ball last in play in lowered hand
Speed and urgency just aren’t there
poor ball handling. Bouncy rolls. Didn’t know when to roll balls to other side after game. was too busy drying viewer seats instead of being where she was supposed to be on court when warmup started.

Great first day. Improved things that I asked her to work on such as awarness and presenting. Was very impressed with her showing her hands to the net after each point. She also did a good job with a 20 point tiebreak as well
She seemed like she didn’t want to be here.  Didn’t really listen to comments.
This kid is a weird one. He's actually decently athletic and knows what's going on-court okay for a rook. But he just starts some absolutely bizarre decisions right
Great athleticism and hands. Got lost a few times in the tiebreaker

Luke is a very good back. He's very enthusiastic and very much wants to do well. He asks for corrective criticism and processes information well. He behaves well on and off court. Very good kid. Good back and wants to be here.
Only on one shift but was hustling extremely hard. Far too many back-back switches and nearly resulted in collision (carolyn contributed as well)

8/1: Super great rolls, really good balancing, great intensity/energy throughout all shifts durung the day. He was partnered with Ethan and they were working really well together/making a great team. He messed up on the one tiebreaker we had yesterday (tried to give the balls to the non-serving player, didn't know how the points went/where the balls were supposed to go). But he took feedback on that really well and noticed me at the net telling him to roll the balls. Broke freeze frame one time on the last shift of the day. Otherwise no other mistakes and I think an extremely strong rookie back with lots of potential.
Day 3 - really high effort! Super quick to serve the player after a point ends.
Extremely high effort and the one thing that causes some minor issues
Looks good out there. High effort. Asking questions on how to get better too. Needs to get a little more confident, balancing can improve. Above average rookie
No terrible mistakes, but just kinda slow all around. Needs to finish his runs which I fed back to him

looks pretty good right now. Decisive on a middle ball that was hers for sure but Benjy started hard at it just in case and she didn’t hesitate! I’ve always seen her as a step below the top of her class, she’s coming into her own right now, I think she’s catching up.
clearly a step behind in a really impressive team. Hesitant, unsure of himself pretty clearly. Looks like a nervous new rookie, should get better.

athletic, figured out to stop rolling the ball when the back wasn’t looking on court 4 since it was going into the wet grass. Running backwards back to his position, needs to turn and run back, not max effort. Solid rookie but some maturity concerns for me as well that make me nervous behaviorally.

he looks great out there. Really knows what’s going on, had a great tiebreaker. Moves well, learning quickly, excellent for a first day ballkid. Now just caught an ace going straight at him and just caught a ball while sitting out that was headed for the water. Really looking aware. Mobility is quite good also, not crazy fast but he can move well. Was my back partner for a while, I was impressed.
sweet sweet snag on a missed first serve. Also he just threw a mahomes quick pitch to get the balance off in time. He’s in mid-tournament form in his 3rd shift of the day

looks good, pleasantly surprised by him so far! Good decision making when he had a ball, ball went to receiving side and game ended. Was decisive in getting fan/umbrella up, clearly knew the score and player first.

good rookie! Awareness is good, she’s looking both ways, checking with receiving side back frequently. Still getting the hang of when to cross but this is improving as shift progresses. Rolling could improve some but not terrible. Awareness definitely the headliner tho, really good for a 2nd day 1st shift rookie, she’s gonna be good.

proactive in balancing but doesn’t have it quite down. Making good decisions when to pick up a ball vs hold to feed player. High effort. Really like that he’s stepping out to the player when he towels off on the far side.

he’s looking really good! He’s compensating for a weak partner very well. Hands aren’t great, but effort is really high. Holding ball last in play down. High awareness. Like what he just did: just looked to his back, expecting a balance since he only had 1 ball. Rookie back didn’t balance (he only had 2) and Maddocks quickly started checking the rest of the court for the 6th ball (still on receiving side back for some reason). He was not satisfied assuming the ball was accounted for with someone else which I like. I don’t usually give 4 stars to 2nd years on the second day but he’s been that good.

decent hands in the warm-up. A few slight hesitations but mostly good decision making. Super high effort and good mover. He’s paying attention when he’s off too, save a ball from under the stands and another that went into the empty stands.
adapted to our net procedures quickly. Vynnie is a really good ballkid, never effort issues, always paying attention.
Susan- she’s a VERY efficient balancer, she knows exactly what’s happening at all times, communicating to the other team what the match situation is at team switch. She’s great

rolls aren’t great. Nice catch. LOVE the communication she just had with a net! Ball in middle of court bouncing high in that no man’s land area, player went to other back for balls, she called off the net with her hand, went and got the ball from the court all while staying locked in in the player if they turned to her, this was really really well done!
high effort, a bit hesitant at times but mostly good. Good rolls. He looks a lot better than I remember from last year. Small sample size, maybe just caught a good few games but liked what I saw!
He looks really good out there. excellent throwing balances. Quick/decisive first move. I don’t really have anything negative to say, he’s getting really really good.

still learning our net rules, poaching a good number of balls and causing some confusion with her net partner (only reason I knock her down from a 3.5) Good awareness, great rolls, great speed/athleticism.

too eager to get the ball. Getting a little Lawrenz-y where he’s a little over agressive and causing confusion for his back partner. Everything else looks good it’s just a little too much and it’s noticeable.
didn’t realize game was over, tried to feed player. Number of similar type things where they weren’t huge mistakes but lack of situational awareness
already holding ball in play down consistently! She looks fantastic for a second day rookie, need to see more to feel comfortable with a 3 rating but she was good!
Balancing well. Looks aware. good job jumping out in front of player to receive balls from net. Makes good decisions but needs to get a little more crisp for a 3.5

hesitating slightly at on balls down the middle and could take a few more steps off the back while servicing a player. Other than that she looks like she knows what she’s doing, good decisions and awareness.
forgot to serve player on let, then threw him the same ball he’d just served. Somewhat indecisive, not surprising for a 2nd day rookie. Not bad, expect improvement. Held her own in tiebreak.
doing a good job stepping out to serve player/receive balls back. She looks confident out there. Locked in tiebreak. Considered a 4, I’m not quite there yet but she really looks good.

a little late to pick up on players weird serve tendencies but never got totally caught out before he figured it out. Good at communication with his back partner. Not flashy just gets the job done well. In control in tiebreak.
rolled behind player, it was close but he realized his mistake immediately. Mostly good, can get a little poachy at times. Situationally aware. Could argue 3.5 but I want to see a little more
good effort! Needed to show hands a little more in tiebreak but fine, and did well on the tiebreak otherwise. Really liked what I saw from him in a short shift.
just going too fast. Fumble and partially tripped over a bag trying to go too fast. Will run out of position at times. Everything else is great
Bouncy bouncy rolls. He knows what he’s doing but there are errors mixed in fairly often.

looks solid still some hesitancy. tried to switch balls after first game of match, back caught it tho. Unnecessary run to back. Did ok with a very weak net partner. Looks like an ok second year. Love his enthusiasm/commitment tho!

fine on ball-in-net operations but situational awareness is quite bad. rookie back had to get her attention for balls switching sides. Then didn’t realize a game ended again in next game. 3rd consecutive game not noticing game ended, back eventually got her attention. Bouncy bouncy rolls.

net tried to get him to switch balls after 1st game of match, he called him off! Ready to serve player after let. Looks surprisingly comfortable out there for such a  young first year! Failed to serve player first a few times when ball was coming to him, this needs to be fixed but otherwise really good!
Mens singles final quality net. He runs back or across when he doesn’t need to sometimes. But other than that, he’s fast, high intensity, good awareness, got everything you want
great athlete. Good throwing balances. Situational awareness is good. Would like to see her step further from back when serving player, and start thinking more about pre-balancing.
good balancer, throws are crisp, he looks very confident out there.really good second year

rolls can be wildly inconsistent at times. Just sent one right between the two backs, most aren’t that bad tho. Fairly aware, maybe a bet too jumpy at times? He should get better but we gotta fix those rolls

looks like a more nervous rookie. Inconsistent with balancing, just not quite upto the pace of play yet. I think she’ll be fine when she loses the nervous, she seems to be second guessing. Good effort tho and athleticism.

HIGH EFFORT. Really looking like he knows what he’s doing, decisive. Dropped one ball trying to scoop another and he was disgusted with himself even tho it cost him very little time, like how much he wants to do well out there. Was surprised by a ball change but did hear the ump say it, just didn’t know before. Looking really confident is the main takeaway.
looks ok. forgot to check his back for a few seconds, just rolled right in front of a player coming back from towel. Looks mostly comfortable, just needs to cut down on the mistakes.
he looks excellent out there, he’s got our net rules down pat already. So fast, good decisions and court aware.
